media.ext_alt_text: Chloride-bearing terrain on Mars does not stand out in regular black-and-white images but shows up a distinct pink to purple hue in colour-infrared images, making ESA's ExoMars CaSSIS instrument a unique tool to study the distribution of salts across Mars from orbit. In the absence of colour, chlorides are easily confused with other light-toned materials such as clays. This image shows a salt deposit in a crater with the appearance of a 'smiley'.

results.text: Is this accurate? @CommunityNotes

footer.text: Context is written by people who use X, and appears when rated helpful by others.  Find out more.

summary.text:
At the time, yes. Facebook concluded their algorithim was in error because it was "race-blind" and was detecting what Facebook considered to be less harmful content like "men are pigs".

The article heading stems from a 2021 Washington Post article.

https://web.archive.org/web/20221107135503/https://en-volve.com/2021/12/04/facebooks-race-blind-algorithm-backfires-in-their-face-finds-90-of-hate-speech-was-directed-toward-white-people-and-men/

https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2021/11/21/facebook-algorithm-biased-race/
